name: Barcode/QR Detection from Photos (Vision)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Barcode/QR detection from still images (Vision)
Use this when the user wants to detect barcodes/QR codes from a photo (still frame), NOT live metadata scanning.

APPROACH:
- Use PhotosUI PhotosPicker (preferred) to import an image.
- Convert to CGImage and run Vision VNDetectBarcodesRequest.
- Return all detected barcodes with: symbology, payloadStringValue, confidence, and boundingBox for overlays.

CRITICAL UX REQUIREMENT:
- Always show BOTH:
  1) the raw payload (exact payloadStringValue)
  2) a normalized "display" value suitable for humans
- Copy button should copy the normalized value by default, but provide a "Copy raw" option (or a details view).

NORMALIZATION (carrier-aware, fixes FedEx/USPS/UPS label codes):
- Shipping labels (often Code 128 / PDF417) frequently encode routing/Application Identifiers. The decoded payload may be longer than the tracking number.
- Implement carrier-aware normalization that produces a PRIMARY display value plus OPTIONAL labeled candidates.
  - Keep the raw payload always.
  - Build helpers:
    - digitsOnly = raw.filter { $0.isNumber }
    - upperAlnum = raw.uppercased().filter { $0.isLetter || $0.isNumber }
  - UPS: if upperAlnum starts with "1Z" and has >= 18 chars, candidate = first 18 (label: "UPS (1Z)")
  - USPS: if digitsOnly is exactly 22 digits, candidate = digitsOnly (label: "USPS (22 digits)")
  - FedEx: if digitsOnly is exactly 12 digits, candidate = digitsOnly (label: "FedEx (12 digits)")
          if digitsOnly is exactly 15 digits, candidate = digitsOnly (label: "FedEx (15 digits)")
  - Ambiguous lengths (like 20 digits) should be labeled as "Carrier (20 digits)" — do NOT duplicate it as both USPS and FedEx.
  - If digitsOnly is longer than 22:
    - Compute last22 = suffix(22). If it starts with "9" (strong USPS signal), treat it as USPS and suppress shorter suffix candidates (like last20/last15/last12) to reduce noise.
    - Otherwise, generate suffix candidates (last 22, last 20, last 15, last 12) with clear labels.
- UI: show the primary value prominently and list candidates below; let users tap a candidate to copy it.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT assume the decoded string is already user-friendly.
- Do NOT permanently strip prefixes without preserving the raw payload.
- Do NOT hardcode "last 12" for every label — use carrier-aware candidates.
- Do NOT use StoreKit/Apple Pay/IAP just because the app mentions "payments" as ledger entries.
antiPatterns: Assuming payloadStringValue is always the value users want to copy (shipping labels often embed extra prefixes)
Stripping prefixes without preserving the raw payload value somewhere in the UI
Using Vision VNDetectBarcodesRequest for live camera scanning (use AVCaptureMetadataOutput for real-time)
Treating ledger "payments" as Apple Pay / StoreKit purchases and adding entitlements
